title: Run Meta Layer — Heatmap, Echo Whisper & Graveyard Study
problem: Between runs, the player has no diagnostic feedback (where do I keep dying?), no ambient presence from past runs (Ghost Echo is a silent stat bonus), and no permanent meta-progression beyond XP and stash items. The graveyard is a memorial with no utility beyond echo invocation. Long-term players hit a ceiling where every run feels stateless.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: persistent meta systems orthogonal to core death/graveyard loop — three new between-run layers

analysis: Three independent meta systems targeting different retention gaps. (1) Death Floor Heatmap: players accumulate deaths but have no diagnostic view of where they keep dying. A per-floor death counter exposed as a bar chart closes the feedback loop and makes the Graveyard screen useful between runs, not just post-death. (2) Echo Whisper: Ghost Echo grants 25% stat bonus but is silent — it misses the narrative opportunity of a mentor figure. A one-time whisper per run on floor 1 transforms a passive bonus into a presence. (3) Graveyard Study: every graveyard item is currently either an echo source or dead weight. Study introduces a permanent sacrifice decision — trade echo potential for lasting knowledge — adding a between-run meta layer that compounds across many runs.
implementation: gameStore.ts: floorDeaths: number[] added to CareerStats (index = absFloor-1, incremented in endRun on death). echoWhisperShown: boolean added to GameState (reset each startRun), markEchoWhisperShown() action. GraveyardEntry.studied?: boolean field. careerStats.studiedBonuses: string[] (quality key per studied entry). studyGraveyardEntry(uid) action marks entry as studied and appends quality string. GraveyardScreen.tsx: FloorHeatmap component renders 30 bars (red, height proportional to death count, floor labels every 5) above the entry list. EntryCard gains STUDY ✦ button when not studied and not active echo; shows studied bonus description inline after study. GridScreen.tsx: ECHO_WHISPERS lookup table (11 slot-keyed strings). useEffect fires 2.5s after mount when floor===1 && ghostCharm active && !echoWhisperShown — renders purple italic whisper text overlay for 6s.

decisions.decision: Graveyard Study is irreversible — studying an entry permanently marks it and prevents echo invocation
decisions.rationale: The value of the study mechanic comes from the decision: echo (temporary floor power) vs. knowledge (permanent record). If study were reversible the choice would have no weight. The sacrifice framing mirrors the graveyard's existing death-penalty philosophy.
alternativesConsidered: Study as a copy (keep entry + gain bonus) — removes the tension entirely
decisions.recommendation: Mark studied: true, prevent echo from studied entries. Stack-agnostic: purely a state flag, no engine dependency.

decisions.decision: Study bonuses are flavor-only in v1 — descriptions shown in Graveyard, no mechanical effect
decisions.rationale: Mechanical effects (e.g. extended fog radius, auto-identification) require changes across multiple engine files and need balance testing. Shipping flavor bonuses first validates the core decision mechanic without risking balance. Effects can be layered in v2 once the careerStats.studiedBonuses[] array is populated with real player data.
alternativesConsidered: Ship 1-2 mechanical bonuses with flavor — increases scope and bug surface in a pre-App Store window
decisions.recommendation: Flavor descriptions keyed by item quality (5 tiers). Revisit mechanical effects post-launch.

decisions.decision: Echo Whisper fires once per run, on floor 1 only, with a 2.5s delay
decisions.rationale: Firing on floor 1 gives the whisper context — the player just invoked the echo and is about to explore. The 2.5s delay ensures the player has settled into the grid view before the text appears. Once-per-run prevents the whisper from becoming intrusive noise across floors.
alternativesConsidered: Fire on every floor with echo active — too frequent, loses the 'mentor speaks rarely' atmosphere
Fire immediately on mount — competes with existing mount effects (legend hint, ghost echo hint)
decisions.recommendation: 2.5s delay, floor 1 only, echoWhisperShown flag prevents repeat. 6s display duration.

successCriteria: Death heatmap renders in GraveyardScreen after first death, correctly shows floor index for each bar. Echo whisper appears on floor 1 when ghost echo active, does not repeat within the same run. Study button appears on unstudied entries, marks them permanently after tap, shows quality-keyed description. TypeScript 0 errors. No regressions in graveyard or GridScreen behaviour.
knownLimitations: Study bonuses are flavor-only — no mechanical effect shipped in v1
Whisper strings are hardcoded slot-keyed lookup (11 entries) — no dynamic generation from run history
Heatmap only shows up to 30 floors (absoluteFloor 1-30); tiers beyond tier 3 are not visualised
userGuide:
## Death Floor Heatmap
The Graveyard screen now shows a bar chart of your deaths across all 30 dungeon floors. Taller red bars mean you die there most. Use it to identify your danger zone and change strategy for those floors.

## Echo Whisper
When Ghost Echo is active on floor 1 of a run, the echo speaks once — a short italic line from the character whose item you carry. It appears 2-3 seconds after you enter the dungeon and fades after 6 seconds. Each item slot has a different whisper.

## Graveyard Study
Every item in the graveyard has two uses: **Invoke Echo** (temporary power for one floor) or **Study** (permanent knowledge). Studying consumes the item — it can no longer be echoed — and unlocks a permanent insight shown in the Graveyard screen. Study bonuses are quality-keyed: studying a Unique unlocks different knowledge than studying a Normal.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition — Run Meta Layer implemented and verified

publicInterface.name: CareerStats.floorDeaths
publicInterface.description: Indexed by absFloor-1; incremented on death in endRun

publicInterface.name: CareerStats.studiedBonuses
publicInterface.description: Quality IDs of studied graveyard entries — currently flavor, future gate

publicInterface.name: GraveyardEntry.studied
publicInterface.description: True after studyGraveyardEntry is called for this entry

publicInterface.name: studyGraveyardEntry
publicInterface.description: Marks entry as studied and appends quality id to careerStats.studiedBonuses

publicInterface.name: FloorHeatmap
publicInterface.description: 30-bar chart of floorDeaths[], shown in GraveyardScreen above entry list

publicInterface.name: STUDY_BONUS_DESC
publicInterface.description: Quality-keyed flavor descriptions of knowledge gained by studying an item

codeSnippets.label: Death heatmap bar chart (30 floors)
studyGraveyardEntry action in gameStore

implementationNotes: floorDeaths[] is indexed by absFloor-1 (0-based). The array can have gaps (floors never visited) — FloorHeatmap fills missing indices with 0 via the Array.from pattern.
FloorHeatmap uses a fixed 30-bar width regardless of actual progression depth — this makes the chart layout stable and non-reflowing as the player progresses.
studiedBonuses[] stores quality IDs (strings), not UIDs. Studying two magic items stores 'magic' twice — future consumers will need to decide if the bonus stacks or was already applied.

A studied entry can never be invoked as Ghost Echo — the study button only renders when !isActive, and invokeGhostEcho only reads entries where !studied. These are enforced separately but both must hold.
